fields.description:
We need a notification service that informs merchants about payment state changes so that merchants stop polling our status API every second.

The current polling approach is overloading the status endpoint and customers want real-time updates.

Acceptance Criteria

  • Merchant receives a webhook within 5 seconds of a payment state change
  • Failed webhook deliveries are retried with exponential backoff up to 24 hours
  • All notification payloads are signed with HMAC-SHA256

Given a payment moves to SETTLED
When the notification service processes the event
Then the merchant webhook endpoint receives exactly one signed notification

The service must not lose events during deploys. It should handle 2000 events per second at peak.

See PAY-1388 for the schema discussion.

comments.body: For the event backbone, let's use Kafka. It already runs in prod for the ledger service.
Build #4712 succeeded. Artifacts uploaded to nexus.

Kafka is good. We considered RabbitMQ but rejected it because we already operate Kafka and don't want a second broker.

We also decided to use Redis for the delivery-attempt cache instead of Memcached.

One concern: the retry storm after a merchant outage could be a performance risk for the egress proxy. We should cap concurrent deliveries per merchant.

Also this is blocked by the PLAT-77 service mesh upgrade — the egress proxy only supports mTLS after that lands.

Saw this in staging while testing the consumer:

2026-05-04 10:58:12 ERROR NotificationConsumer - delivery failed for merchant m-2231
java.net.SocketTimeoutException: connect timed out
    at java.base/sun.nio.ch.NioSocketImpl.timedFinishConnect(NioSocketImpl.java:546)
    at java.base/sun.nio.ch.NioSocketImpl.connect(NioSocketImpl.java:597)
    at java.base/java.net.SocksSocketImpl.connect(SocksSocketImpl.java:327)
    at okhttp3.internal.connection.RealConnection.connectSocket(RealConnection.kt:295)
    at okhttp3.internal.connection.RealConnection.connect(RealConnection.kt:207)
    at okhttp3.internal.connection.ExchangeFinder.findConnection(ExchangeFinder.kt:226)
    at okhttp3.internal.connection.ExchangeFinder.findHealthyConnection(ExchangeFinder.kt:106)
    at okhttp3.internal.connection.RealCall.initExchange$okhttp(RealCall.kt:255)
    at okhttp3.internal.connection.ConnectInterceptor.intercept(ConnectInterceptor.kt:32)
    at okhttp3.internal.http.RealInterceptorChain.proceed(RealInterceptorChain.kt:109)
    at io.pay.notify.delivery.WebhookDeliveryClient.post(WebhookDeliveryClient.java:88)
    at io.pay.notify.delivery.DeliveryWorker.run(DeliveryWorker.java:61)
Caused by: java.net.ConnectException: Connection refused
    at java.base/sun.nio.ch.Net.pollConnect(Net.java:672)
    at java.base/sun.nio.ch.SocketChannelImpl.finishConnect(SocketChannelImpl.java:946)
2026-05-04 10:58:13 WARN DeliveryWorker - scheduling retry 1/24 for merchant m-2231
2026-05-04 10:58:45 ERROR NotificationConsumer - delivery failed for merchant m-2231
2026-05-04 10:58:45 WARN DeliveryWorker - scheduling retry 2/24 for merchant m-2231
2026-05-04 10:59:52 ERROR NotificationConsumer - delivery failed for merchant m-2231
2026-05-04 10:59:52 WARN DeliveryWorker - scheduling retry 3/24 for merchant m-2231

That's the staging egress firewall, not a code issue. TODO: add the merchant sandbox CIDR to the egress allowlist before UAT.

We still need to write the runbook for on-call.
